name: Integration Gap Closure
summary: Close the remaining internal client/backend wiring gaps surfaced by the audit so only external launch blockers remain.

notes: The alert, referral, delete-account, support, store checkout, Apple Pay, and BLE disconnect gaps found after the QA sweep are now closed in source.
Focused API regression coverage for the integration slice now runs alongside the existing backend-core, realtime-worker-hardening, richer-dashboard, and device-priority sweeps.
Remaining launch blockers are external or manual only: OAuth provider setup, APNs provisioning, and live Stripe or Apple Pay credential validation.

discovered_work: The production QA closure artifact was too optimistic; the audit surfaced internal contract gaps in alerts, referrals, payments, account deletion, support, and BLE disconnect handling.
Closed the launch-path gaps with live alert and referral contracts, persisted support and deletion flows, cart-backed Stripe plus Apple Pay finalization, and BLE disconnect helper wiring.
Validated the slice with a fresh API regression sweep, root type-check and test, launch preflight, and an iOS simulator build.
blockers: Google OAuth browser and iOS client IDs plus redirect configuration still require manual environment and console setup.
Apple Sign-In portal configuration for the service or app identifiers used by web and iOS remains an external prerequisite.
APNs credentials are still required to verify end-to-end push notification delivery outside the simulator.
Live Stripe and Apple Pay verification still depends on external credential and merchant certificate setup.
